title: CString failure in WolfTpmSwtpm::connect returns wrong error variant
description: In wolftpm-tss/src/connection.rs WolfTpmSwtpm::connect, if 'host' or 'port.to_string()' contain a null byte, CString::new returns Err and the code maps it to Error::MalformedResponse. The user-visible error message is 'TPM returned a malformed response header' — which is completely wrong. The actual cause is an invalid argument (null byte in hostname). The Error enum has no InvalidArg variant, so the wrong variant was used. Trigger: caller passes a hostname string containing a null byte.
